response.justification: While the candidate brings strong software engineering fundamentals and 14+ years of experience with system-level performance optimization, they lack the specialized expertise in hardware architecture, SoC/IP modeling, GPU design, and AI accelerator optimization required for a principal-level role in cloud infrastructure hardware. The career trajectory is primarily software-focused (backends, web platforms, embedded systems) rather than hardware-centric, representing a significant domain gap that would require extensive ramp-up.
strengths: Strong foundational CS education (ETH Zürich) and 14+ years of progressive experience demonstrating deep systems thinking across multiple domains
Proven expertise in performance optimization and designing low-latency, high-throughput systems handling millions of requests
Comfortable working with lower-level languages (C++, DLang) and diverse architectural contexts from embedded systems to distributed infrastructure
concerns: No demonstrated experience with SoC, IP modeling, or hardware architecture design—core requirements for the role
No background in GPU architecture, AI accelerators, or hardware-software co-design for performance-critical accelerator workloads
Current work focuses on software development and AI-agent infrastructure rather than hardware optimization, accelerator workload analysis, or DNN training/inference optimization
